channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Semantic Layer Best Practices: 7 Mistakes to Avoid
item.description: Semantic layers don't fail because the technology is wrong. They fail because of design decisions made in the first two weeks — choices that seem reasonable at the time and create compounding...

item.title: I Run MCP Servers. Here's What the Recent Vulnerabilities Actually Mean for Me
item.description: Last week, two MCP security vulnerabilities went public. CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS 9.8) in the nginx-ui MCP endpoint. A STDIO transport design flaw affecting all SDKs, potentially exposing 200,000...

item.title: Phive v1.1.1 — automatic port conflict handling for local VS Code environments
item.description: Phive v1.1.1 is now available. This update improves local development environment stability with automatic port conflict resolution and smarter runtime management. What’s new in v1.1.1: •...

item.title: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026.
item.description: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai in conversation on the I/O 2026 Dialogues stage

item.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro 2 has hit one of its best prices ahead of summer travel
item.description: With Memorial day weekend kicking off the travel season, we’re seeing a lot of deals pop up on travel gadgets, from portable power banks to noise-canceling headphones. One of the best right now is...

item.title: Building a SQL-like Relational Database Engine in C++ From Scratch
item.description: Most of us use databases every day. But at some point I started wondering: How does SQL actually work internally? How are queries parsed? How do joins work? What happens after a SELECT statement?...

item.title: How a Self-Documenting Semantic Layer Reduces Data Team Toil
item.description: Every data team knows documentation is important. And almost every data team has a backlog of undocumented tables, unlabeled columns, and outdated descriptions that nobody has time to fix. The...

item.title: The Adopter: Advocating for OSS You Use (But Don't Own)
item.description: The Adopter is a company that uses an open source technology—it may very likely be a core part of their internal infrastructure—and they engage externally with that project's community. They're...

item.title: Optimizing Vite Build Output: A Practical Guide to Tree-Shaking
item.description: I used to think bundle optimisation was someone else's problem. I'd write code using convenient namespace imports like import * as utils from './utils', run npm run build, and ship whatever came...

item.title: I built a free audit tool that runs 12 checks in parallel against any domain. Here is the architecture.
item.description: I spent the past few months building Canopy Guard, a free website audit tool that combines SEO, AEO, and GEO visibility scoring with a full security posture check. One scan, one report, about 15...

item.title: I made a free 7-video series to prep for the new GH-600 (GitHub Agentic AI Developer) cert
item.description: TL;DR: GitHub's new agentic-AI certification, GH-600 (GitHub Certified: Agentic AI Developer), is in beta until May 31, 2026, with 80% off for the first 100 candidates using promo code...

item.title: Choosing the Right Treasure Map to Avoid Data Decay in Veltrix
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving We thought we were solving the classic event-sourcing problem – storing and replaying events from a distributed system to ensure data consistency. Sounds...

item.title: Meta’s Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
item.description: Meta's new Forum app for iPhones takes Facebook Groups and moves them to a dedicated app with a dedicated AI chatbot to go with it, like an AI revamp of the ill-fated Groups app Facebook shut down...

item.title: Elon, stop trying to make Grok happen
item.description: There is a harsh truth about Elon Musk's "truth-seeking" AI chatbot Grok: It's not very good, and not many people are using it. That's the takeaway of a new Reuters report, which found that Grok...

item.title: The best Memorial Day sales you can shop this weekend
item.description: Memorial Day is nearly here, meaning the seasonal sales are in full swing. If your weekend plans involve pool parties or barbecues, now is a great time to pick up a portable speaker or set of...

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor...

item.title: Govee’s colorful, JBL-tuned Lamp Pro 2 is matching its best price to date
item.description: They say that Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer. I have my doubts given the less-than-balmy weather in many parts of the US this weekend, though that doesn’t mean it’s not an...

item.title: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business ‘fair and square’
item.description: Google officially filed its appeal of the federal ruling deeming it an illegal search monopolist, arguing the decision "crashed" through legal guardrails. "Google just prevailed in the marketplace...

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat actor known as Ghostwriter (aka UAC-0057 and UNC1151Ukraine's National Security and Defense Council) has been observed using lures related to Prometheus, a Ukrainian...

item.title: Waymo suspends freeway driving amid safety concerns
item.description: On Thursday, Waymo customers opened up the app and noticed something unusual: no more freeway service. Trips that would normally take a few minutes on the freeway were suddenly projected to last...

item.title: You can no longer Google the word ‘disregard’
item.description: After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.

item.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
item.description: Google's AI Overviews are running into an interesting problem right now. As of this writing, if you search for the term "disregard," instead of showing the usual AI-generated summary of search...

item.title: We tried Google’s AI glasses and they’re almost there
item.description: Google demoed prototype Android XR glasses that overlay Gemini-powered translation, navigation, and other information directly into your field of view.

item.title: Specialization Beats Scale: A Strategic Variable Most AI Procurement Decisions Overlook
Even If You Hate AI, You Will Use Google AI Search
item.description: The search giant’s AI-crafted answers are so convenient, you’ll be sucked in—to the detriment of the web and the artists and thinkers behind it.

item.title: LG’s 77-inch B5 OLED TV is down to $1,500 and comes with a $200 gift card
item.description: Best Buy’s Memorial Day sale includes a good deal on a massive OLED TV. The 77-inch LG B5 is down to $1,499.99 (originally $2,999.99), and the purchase includes a $200 Best Buy gift card. I’m a...

item.title: The Trump phone is not here
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. It's now been more than a week since Trump Mobile...

item.title: CISA Security Leak
item.description: Crazy story: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly...

item.title: The Download: coding’s future, the ‘Steroid Olympics,’ and AI-driven science
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s...

item.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. "Using...
